title: Market position June 2026: three unique axes (write-gate, stale-catch, receipts); Copilot Memory is the convergent threat
summary: Competitive sweep of Kage 2.0.0 June 2026, sourced : UNIQUE with no real competitor — 1 write time citation rejection Copilot Memory validates citations only at recall, default on for Pro since Mar 2026 — the single bigg
body:
Competitive sweep of Kage 2.0.0 (June 2026, sourced): UNIQUE with no real competitor — (1) write-time citation rejection (Copilot Memory validates citations only at recall, default-on for Pro since Mar 2026 — the single biggest threat; nobody else gates at write), (2) change-time stale-catch at commit/PR (zero competitors for memories; Swimm proves the pattern for docs at ~$29/mo), (3) per-recall token/$ receipts (nothing in any category attributes savings to a specific recall). BEST-IN-COMBINATION: Truth Report — CodeScene does knowledge-maps/bus-factor deeper (€27/seat, Pro-only, not agent-facing); GitClear owns AI-clone research ($12-26/dev); doc-lies rivals are hobby tools (README Clew, Driftless). MERELY COMPETITIVE: staleness-aware recall (Copilot's JIT validation + 28-day expiry ≈80% of value, free); code graph is now COMMODITY (code-graph-mcp, CodeGraph, GitNexus, Sourcegraph MCP GA — tree-sitter+MCP graphs everywhere; differentiate on memory-graph coupling only). BEHIND: distribution (MemPalace 43K stars in days; Letta mindshare; Zep temporal rigor). Positioning that survives: NOT 'memory for agents' (commodity) — 'verified memory with visible ROI'. Durable deltas vs Copilot: write-time strictness, SHA-level fingerprints, team/PR boundary, receipts, open + agent-agnostic. Cursor REMOVED Memories (2.1.x) in favor of Rules.
Verified by: 37-source web sweep 2026-06-12: github.blog changelogs, CodeScene/GitClear pricing pages, mem0/Zep/Letta/ByteRover docs, code-graph-mcp/GitNexus repos
